violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Cheese was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Cheese was above fill line in prep unit. Cheese was packaged and moved to walk in cooler. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of staff rice was in walk in cooler in sealed plastic containers. Rice was 51DF, rice was cooked 3 hours prior, rice was placed in walk in freezer for rapid cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Soup was placed directly into hot holding for re-heating. Soup did not reach proper reheating temperature of 165 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours or less. Corrective Action - Soup was voluntarily disposed of. Correct procedures for re-heating were reviewed with manager. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

(Raw chicken) was observed held at (44) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrective action, chicken was not in unit for more than 30 minutes and was moved into walk in cooler for rapid cooling. The unit was checked and held an ambient temperature of below 41 degrees. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024
